course_description:
The course teaches further Latin grammar and syntax, and studies selections from Latin authors. It involves both a literary and linguistic study of the writers concerned, together with their social and historical significance. NOTE: Instruction by small group teaching.

LATN2230 continues the study of the grammar and syntax of classical Latin, with an emphasis on learning through reading. It is designed for students who have successfully completed (with a grade of 4 or above) LATN1110 and LATN2120 or students who have completed Latin to Year 12 level. If you are in doubt about your situation, please contact the Course Co-ordinator before enrolling. There are 3 hours of classes per week. Regular attendance at classes, preparation, revision and private study are essential for this course.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:

UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Attendance is vitally important for all language classes, important and a supportive environment will be provided so that students can participate in readings, discussion and by asking and answering questions. Lines of translation will be assigned to students for class translation. Microsoft Teams will be used in this course for announcements and communication. Please ensure that you have access in Week 1.

learning_outcomes.description: Read and analyse the reading passages in Jones & Sidwell, Reading Latin: Text up to the end of Section 5F.
Demonstrate via exercises and parsing an understanding of the morphology and syntax of the accompanying material in Jones & Sidwell, Reading Latin: Grammar etc., up to the end of Section 5F.

Complete an authentic assessment assignment(s) to demonstrate the acquisition of advanced digital literacy skills in alignment with UQ's Employability Work Integrated Learning (WIL) and (CLD) Career Development Learning Goals.

assessment_details.title: In Person Mid-Semester Exam
assessment_details.due_date: 15/04/2026 11:00 am The mid-semester exam will take place in class
assessment_details.task_description: Mid Semester Exam translation from Latin into English of passages from Sections 4Eii-5B of the Reading Latin Text parsing to show your understanding of the grammar, syntax and vocabulary of the texts translated weekly in class, so it is important that you do your weekly translations and come to class to translate and engage in discussion translation into English of sentences/passages based on the learning vocabulary and grammar of Sections Introduction-5B grammatical exercises of the type covered in class and for assignments based on the Introduction- 5B, but concentrating on 4E-5B, designed to test your increasing knowledge of the grammar, syntax, and vocabulary covered in these sections. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course Read full task description Hide full task description

Please note that course coordinators cannot approve deferred mild-semester exams via email, Microsoft Teams, or verbally.

assessment_details.title: Skill Building 1: Class Participation and Practice
assessment_details.task_description: Students are required to translate set texts assigned before class, as listed by Section in the Course Profile and on Blackboard. These texts will be re translated and discussed in class in conjunction with points of grammar, syntax, literary style, and where relevant, historical and/or cultural context, and will also be re-examined in the mid-semester and final exam. Each student will also be assigned a specific set of lines to translate individually in class, which will be assigned in week 1 and throughout the semester. Points of grammar, syntax, and vocabulary acquisition will also be reinforced though class exercises which are designed to provide immediate feedback, focus on points of grammar and syntax, literary technique, and relevant historical and/or cultural contexts. Students are encouraged to use the class time to ask questions encountered during preparation or that arise in class during translation, discussion, or during the completion of class exercises. Although AI and Machine translation could potentially be used for this task, at beginner level it does not help students to master the skills needed, and in some cases may be misleading and time consuming. Students are advised to use the specific Learning Materials have been developed for this course, which will be explained in Week 1 of the course. Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students are required to be in class to translate their set lines: written work cannot be submitted in lieu of in-person attendance. Please print out your translated lines and hand them in at the beginning of class: while these will not be assigned an individual mark as this exercise is about providing feedback through discussion, and they form part of your overall participation mark. In person class participation is demonstrated through active participation in all components of the class: demonstration of prepared translations of set texts for weekly classes on Wednesday and Friday, in-class translation and discussion of set lines of the prepared texts, active engagement in discussions directed by the lecturer, and participation in other class learning activities.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Skill Building 2: Submissions
assessment_details.category: Quiz, Reflection, Translation/ Interpretation, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessment_details.due_date: Assignment 1 12/03/2026 2:00 pm Assignment 2 26/03/2026 2:00 pm Assignment 3 30/04/2026 2:00 pm Assignment 4 14/05/2026 2:00 pm Assignment 5 21/05/2026 2:00 pm Option A Continuous Quizzes 12/06/2026 2:00 pm Option B Timed Quiz 12/06/2026 2:00 pm See Blackboard for details
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Longitudinal. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Part 1: 20% Students are required to complete five (5) Skill-Building Assignments. Each assignment will be averaged (i.e. each assignment will contribute equally) for the final grade of 20% The due dates are available under the assessment tab on the LATN2230 Blackboard site. Please note that ALL Five (5) assignments must be submitted to achieve a passing grade for this course. If you are having difficulty meeting a deadline, please consult with Dr McWilliam as soon as possible. Assignments comprise: translation from Latin into English of sentences and passages based on the learning vocabulary, grammar, and syntax studied in the week(s) preceding each assignment parsing of words to show your understanding of the vocab, grammar, and syntax of the text translation into Latin of some simple sentences and/or a short unseen passage based on the learning vocabulary, and grammatical exercises of the type covered in class using the learning vocabulary from LATN1110, LATN2120 and LATN2230. All translations into English must demonstrate your understanding of the Latin Assignments are designed to test your increasing and cumulative knowledge of the grammar, syntax and vocabulary covered during the course, and mastered previously in LATN1110 and LATN2120 It is important to read the feedback you receive each week, reflect on what you need to work on, and ask questions if you are still unsure. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course Part 2: QUIZZES 15% Select Option A 1236 uqlatin quizzes on UQ Extend OR Option B 1 x timed blackboard quiz held in conjunction with the final exam Option A: uqlatin quizzes: Students must complete 3 genuine attempts at all the following types of quizzes (located on UQ EXTEND) with their overall number of quizzes attempted equalling or surpassing 1236 (there are 412 quizzes in total) for all sections of the course 4E(ii)-5F(ii): noun declension adjective/pronoun declension verb conjugation and identification word matching/vocab parsing translation/identification of prepositions and idiomatic constructions If you elect to take this option, you must start your quiz practice in Week 1 and plan your time wisely, or you will not reach the required number of quizzes by the end of semester. STUDENTS WHO DO NOT TAKE UP THIS OPTION MUST COMPLETE THE SINGLE TIMED BLACKBOARD QUIZ HELD IN CONJUNCTION WITH THE FINAL EXAM IN THE EXAM PERIOD Option B: Timed Blackboard Quiz Students who do not complete the uqlatin quiz option must complete ONE timed 10 min Blackboard Quiz held in conjunction with the Final Exam. See the assessment section of Blackboard for details. Only one attempt is permitted. noun declension adjective/pronoun declension verb conjugation word matching/vocab translation translation/identification of prepositions and idiomatic constructions These assessment tasks evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Marking criteria and/or marking rubrics are available in the ‘Assessment’ folder in Blackboard for this course Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
online via UQ Extend or Blackboard. See Blackboard for details
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Assignments submitted late will be marked. Please note that a genuine attempt at answering all questions must be made for all assignments. An assignment which is blank will not be counted as a genuine attempt.
Please note that it is not normally possible to extend or defer the Option A, the continuous uqlatin quizzes on Extend unless you have been keeping up all semester, so please plan your time. If you have started but not kept up with the uqlatin quizzes, please take the timed blackboard quiz held in conjunction with the final exam in Exam Week 1.
If you have been granted a deferral for the final exam, please contact Dr McWilliam and she will provide access to the timed Blackboard Quiz.
Late submission
Please see Blackboard for further details
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: Please see Blackboard for further details

learning_activities.topic: (Week 1) Grammar Section 4E Ablative absolute, cum + subjunctive, pluperfect subjunctive Texts 4E(ii)-(iii) Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss on both Wednesday and Friday
(Week 2) Grammar Section 4F and Texts Section 4F(i) & 4F(ii). Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss on both Wednesday and Friday
(Week 3) 4G Grammar & Texts Section 4G(i)& 4G(ii) Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss in class on both Wednesday and Friday
(Week 4) Grammar H. Text 4H Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss in class on both Wednesday and Friday
(Week 5) Grammar Section 5 and Texts 5A (i)-(iii) Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss in class on both Wednesday and Friday

(Week 6) Grammar 5B and Texts 5B(i)-5B(iii) Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss 5B(i) and (ii) on Wednesday. No class on Good Friday 3rd April
(Week 7) Wednesday & Friday finish 5B(ii)-(iii) Texts Start 5C Grammar Wednesday 15th Mid-Semester Exam
(Week 8) Grammar 5C Cont Texts 5C (i)- (iii). Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss in class on both Wednesday and Friday
(Week 9) Grammar 5D and Texts 5D (i)-(iii) Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss in class on both Wednesday and Friday
(Week 10) Grammar 5E and Texts 5D(iv) 5E(i)& 5E(ii). Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss in class on both Wednesday and Friday
(Week 11) Grammar 5F; Text 5F(i) & 5F (ii). Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss in class on both Wednesday and Friday
(Week 12) Finish Grammar 5F; and any unread texts Please have your texts translated at the beginning of the week and be prepared to translate and discuss in class on both Wednesday and Friday
